# DOT AI OS LLM Guide

Purpose: guide agents implementing or customizing the `dot-ai-os` template: an
agent-native operator workspace on Cloudflare SvelteKit with module-backed
identity, RBAC, audit, file, contact, work-packet, and support surfaces plus
template-owned starter workflows adapted from Jimmy Dashboard OS.

## Read First

- `microservices.template.json`
- `microservices.config.json`
- `microservices.lock.json`
- `README.agent.md`
- `docs/api-boundary.md`

## Modules

- `auth` — signing keys and token verification
- `identity` — email-code login/session primitives
- `org-team-rbac` — workspace org, memberships, roles, invitations, authorize
- `admin-shell` — super-admin table gateway over module tables
- `audit-log` — append-only event trail
- `customer` — contacts and stakeholder records
- `invoice` — work packets/value tracking in this starter
- `support-ticket` — support inbox/open loops
- `file-media` — file metadata and R2 object storage
- `jobs-workflows` — async job slot
- `notifications-inapp` — in-app notification slot

## Architecture Rule

SvelteKit route files are adapters. Domain/API logic lives in detached module use
cases. Template-owned workflow pages (`/app/tasks`, `/app/focus`,
`/app/calendar`, `/app/review`, `/app/knowledge`, `/app/content`,
`/app/ai-team`) use `src/lib/os-data.ts` sample data and should stay thin until a
dedicated module or documented D1 table owns their data.

```txt
src/routes/signup/+page.server.ts          -> createOrganization
src/routes/app/+layout.server.ts           -> resolvePermissions gate
src/routes/app/team/+page.server.ts        -> inviteMember / updateMemberRole
src/routes/app/customers/+page.server.ts   -> listCustomers / upsertCustomer
src/routes/app/invoices/+page.server.ts    -> listInvoices / createInvoice
src/routes/app/files/+page.server.ts       -> listFiles / createUploadTicket
src/routes/admin/[resource]/+page.server.ts -> listRecords
```

## Approval Gates

Require user approval before changing:

- secrets
- remote migrations
- calendar OAuth or write-back
- Hermes ingestion or Obsidian export
- payment/email provider behavior
- Cloudflare resources
- remote deployments
- forked module internals
- AI-provider calls or prompt pipelines

Local baseline commands may apply the checked-in D1 migrations to Wrangler's
local database:

```bash
pnpm microservices local migrate
pnpm microservices local dev
pnpm microservices local smoke
```
